description: Selected governance-relevant articles from the EU General Data Protection Regulation.

requirements.title: Principles relating to processing of personal data
requirements.description: Personal data shall be processed lawfully, fairly and in a transparent manner; collected for specified, explicit and legitimate purposes; adequate, relevant and limited to what is necessary; accurate and kept up to date; kept for no longer than necessary; and processed with appropriate security.

requirements.title: Data protection by design and by default
requirements.description: The controller shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures for ensuring that, by default, only personal data which are necessary for each specific purpose of the processing are processed.

requirements.title: Security of processing
requirements.description: The controller and the processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including pseudonymisation, encryption, confidentiality, integrity, availability, and resilience of processing systems.

requirements.title: Notification of a personal data breach to the supervisory authority
requirements.description: In the case of a personal data breach, the controller shall without undue delay and, where feasible, not later than 72 hours after having become aware of it, notify the personal data breach to the supervisory authority.

requirements.title: Data protection impact assessment
requirements.description: Where a type of processing is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller shall carry out an assessment of the impact of the envisaged processing operations on the protection of personal data.
